about.title: About OpenHW Explorer
about.subtitle: A community-built navigator for the OpenHW Group open-source hardware ecosystem.

whatIsThis.title: What is OpenHW Explorer?
whatIsThis.p1: OpenHW Explorer is a community project that helps developers, students, and researchers discover and navigate OpenHW Group open-source RISC-V hardware IP projects. It provides a friendly, searchable interface across processor cores, verification tools, SoC platforms, and more.
whatIsThis.p2: Whether you're a student learning about RISC-V, an engineer evaluating cores for production, or a researcher exploring the ecosystem — this tool helps you find the right starting point.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: OpenHW Explorer is an independent community project and is NOT officially affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by the OpenHW Group or any of its member organizations. The original implementation and initial curation were created by AlexChen. All project data, descriptions, and statistics are collected from publicly available sources (primarily GitHub) and may not reflect the most current state. For official information, please visit openhwgroup.org.

browse.title: Browse Projects
browse.desc: Explore OpenHW projects with category filtering and smart search.

filter.title: Smart Filtering
filter.desc: Filter by role, category, core type, and verification type.

detail.title: Project Details
detail.desc: Detailed pages with stats, links, tags, and related project recommendations.

i18n.title: Bilingual Support
i18n.desc: Full English and Chinese interface for the global RISC-V community.

author.title: About the Author
author.bio: Hi, I'm AlexChen (开源老陈). I built OpenHW Explorer for the community, hoping engineers, students, and researchers can find the right OpenHW starting point faster. I care about open hardware, RISC-V, and practical collaboration - feel free to say hi on LinkedIn.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.content: OpenHW Explorer is open source. If you'd like to report an issue, suggest a feature, or contribute code, you're welcome to visit the project repository.

contributePage.title: Learn and Contribute to OpenHW
contributePage.subtitle: Start with practical learning tracks, then join real OpenHW contribution workflows.

classroom.title: OpenHW Mini Classroom
classroom.subtitle: V1 focuses on guided learning tracks using open resources and real project entry points.

contribution.title: Contribution Onboarding
contribution.desc: Understand ECA, process expectations, and contribution flow before your first PR.

cores.title: Core Deep Dives
cores.desc: Start from flagship and embedded cores to understand architecture trade-offs and usage paths.

verification.title: Verification Workshop
verification.desc: Learn UVM-centric verification flow and instruction generation in the CORE-V ecosystem.

software.title: Software Enablement
software.desc: Bridge hardware and software with SDK and RTOS-oriented project paths.

classroom.futureNote: Roadmap: this section will evolve into themed PPT-based open classes (contribute flow, core usage, verification playbooks) in V2.

whyContribute.title: Why Contribute?
whyContribute.reason1: Impact real-world chip designs used in industry and academia
whyContribute.reason2: Build expertise in RISC-V architecture and verification methodologies
whyContribute.reason3: Join a welcoming community of hardware engineers and researchers
whyContribute.reason4: Get your work recognized in a major open-source hardware organization

howTo.title: How to Contribute
howTo.step1Desc: Browse the repos below and find one that matches your skills and interests.
howTo.step2Desc: Check the project's CONTRIBUTING.md and coding conventions.
howTo.step3Desc: OpenHW projects require an Eclipse Contributor Agreement (ECA).
howTo.step4Desc: Fork, create a branch, make your changes, and submit a PR.

contributePage.goodFirstIssuesDesc: Issues tagged as beginner-friendly across OpenHW repos
contributePage.noGoodFirstIssues: No good first issues found at the moment. Check back later or browse open issues directly on GitHub.

rankings.title: Project Rankings
rankings.disclaimer: For reference only. Rankings are derived from automated GitHub data (stars, recent commits, contributors) and have not been individually verified. Numbers may not fully reflect each project's actual standing — treat this as a directional snapshot, not a definitive ranking.

resources.workGroupsDesc: Join a task group to collaborate with other contributors
